title: Political agreement for the extension of the Hokuriku Shinkansen with a station in Katsura
summary: The ruling LDP and the Restoration Party agree on the route that includes a new station in the Katsura district, Kyoto.

title: Spain defeats France 2-0 and qualifies for the World Cup final after four editions
summary: The Spanish national team, with a solid defense and precise play, advanced to the decisive match of the tournament.

title: Trump withdraws controversial 20% tax on transit through the Strait of Hormuz
summary: The US administration replaces the fee with investment agreements with Persian Gulf countries.

title: Bank of Japan publishes 2016 minutes on the introduction of negative interest rates and their side effects
summary: Documents reveal internal controversies and risks of the unconventional monetary policy promoted by Governor Kuroda.

title: Japanese Parliament begins debate on reform of the Imperial House Law
summary: The opposition criticizes the project for considering it anachronistic and far from public opinion.

title: Wall Street closes positive due to moderation of inflation; IBM suffers a 25% plunge
summary: The Dow Jones added 9 points, while tech stocks advanced, but IBM was the big loser of the day.

title: New York imposes a one-year moratorium on the construction of data centers
summary: The measure responds to concerns about the increase in electricity consumption and the impact on rates.

title: Tokyo's Nikkei index surges more than 1000 points driven by US tech stocks
summary: Japanese semiconductor stocks led the gains, following optimism on Wall Street.

title: Japan's machinery orders fall 12.4% in May, first decline in two months
summary: The figure was a negative surprise, but the government maintains its outlook for economic recovery.

title: Yen strengthens to 162 against the dollar on lower expectations of rate hikes
summary: The Japanese currency benefited from the slowdown in US inflation.

title: Hideki Matsui praises young Japanese baseball prospects at the All-Star Game
summary: The legendary hitter especially highlighted Murakami and Okamoto for their offensive ability.

title: Relatives of Fukui murder victim demand a new trial due to evidence concealment
summary: The mourners maintain that both they and the convicted person were harmed by prosecutorial misconduct.

title: The Hakata Gion Yamakasa festival culminates with the traditional morning race
summary: Dozens of participants carried huge floats through the streets of Fukuoka in the final event.

title: Influencer Reika Miyazaki sentenced to prison for evading 150 million yen in taxes
summary: The Tokyo District Court found her guilty of hiding income in her tax return.

title: UN reports over 2,200 civilian casualties in Ukraine in June, highest figure in four years
summary: Russian attacks on populated areas intensified, increasing the number of dead and wounded.

title: Shohei Ohtani returns to action in the second half of the MLB season
summary: The Japanese star of the Angels will play as a batter, but it is unknown when he will pitch again.

title: Xi Jinping's associates purged in anti-corruption campaign ahead of CPC congress
summary: Several high-ranking officials have been dismissed in a context of growing internal party struggle.

title: Powell assures the Fed will not stop until inflation is controlled, despite signs of slowdown
summary: The Federal Reserve chairman reiterated the commitment to maintain a restrictive policy.

title: Concert organizers reinforce measures against ticket resale with facial recognition
summary: Authorized resale platforms and verification systems seek to prevent ticket speculation.

title: Houthi rebels attack a Saudi airport and threaten to block the Strait of Hormuz
summary: The drone attack raises tensions in the Middle East and could affect global energy trade.
